mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate weakening demand, creating negative momentum in equity markets that manifests as lower S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term falls below 0.05 or 20d returns show positive correlation with the composite demand index.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% drives central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in 70%+ of high-inflation periods despite rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a 10%+ decline signals recovering supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns remain negative after INDPRO rebounds by 10%+ from trough.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k concurrent with declining unemployment indicates a tight labor market, pushing inflation higher and causing S&P 500 returns to turn negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d returns remain positive when PAYEMS exceeds 150k and UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 2%+ quarterly decline in GDP growth signals economic contraction, creating negative momentum in equity markets that manifests as lower S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances with GDPC1 declines > 2% q/q.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate above 4% triggers bond market repricing, leading to higher equity discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.
